divergenceDescription: scenarios.ts:w2-preexec-block (Task D.7): the legacy's W2 pre-execution deterministic-ambiguity block (captureRouteTrees + a Pillar-2 pre-exec catalog gate) fires BEFORE any execute() call and holds the run as 'invalid' (Issue). RunQaUseCase has NO equivalent pre-exec ambiguity gate wired — preExecAmbiguityCatches/deterministicSelectorBlocks are hardcoded to the literal 0 in run-qa.use-case.ts (documented in its own header comment and in the D.5/D.6 apply-progress as an explicit, unwired port gap). Feeding this scenario's deps through RunQaUseCase proceeds past validate() (ok:true) straight into a clean execute() pass, landing on verdict=pass/sideEffect=pr instead of the legacy's verdict=invalid/sideEffect=issue. This is a Task E.0/Slice E port-gap to close later (wiring a real pre-exec ambiguity port), not a bug to fix inside D.7's own scope.
approver: sdd-apply (Plan 6, Task D.7) — pending human/maintainer sign-off before Slice E closes this gap

divergenceDescription: scenarios.ts:codemode-infra-toolchain (Task D.7): the legacy's code-mode compile gate distinguishes a toolchain/infra failure (validation.infra=true, e.g. a missing JAVA_HOME) from a genuine static-gate failure (infra=false/absent) — infra=true routes to verdict=infra-error (no Issue). RunQaUseCase's own validate phase branches ONLY on validation.ok, never reads validation.infra at all (the ValidationPort.validate() return type carries `infra?: boolean` on the wire per ports/index.ts's own comment, 'infra optional: mirrors src/qa/validate.ts CheckResult', but RunQaUseCase's !validation.ok branch does not consume it) — so a code-mode toolchain failure is currently classified verdict=invalid (Issue) instead of the legacy's verdict=infra-error (no Issue). A Task E.0/Slice E consumption gap (wiring RunQaUseCase's validate branch to read validation.infra), not fixed inside D.7's own scope (report the divergence, do not silently patch decision logic during a characterization-harness task).
approver: sdd-apply (Plan 6, Task D.7) — pending human/maintainer sign-off before Slice E closes this gap

divergenceDescription: scenarios.ts:coverage-enforce-improves (Judgment-day D.7 FIX 5): the legacy persists a STALE PRE-improvement coverageRatio (0.25) instead of the scenario's own post-improvement value (1.0) due to a genuine LEGACY BUG, not a design choice — a stale-variable-binding defect. Root cause: `ccForPersistence = cc;` is assigned exactly ONCE at src/pipeline.ts:2930 (BEFORE the enforce-mode improvement regen/re-measure), and is NEVER re-assigned even though the local `cc` variable IS reassigned after the improvement re-measure at src/pipeline.ts:2963 (`cc = computeChangeCoverage(changed, reCollected ?? new Map(), reBranches ?? undefined);`). The persisted telemetry (`ratio` at src/pipeline.ts:3252, derived from `ccForPersistence`) therefore reflects the stale pre-improvement measurement, while the actual PUBLISH DECISION (`coverageStatus`/`blocksPublish`, both re-derived from the fresh post-improvement `cc` at src/pipeline.ts:2964 via decideCoverage(cc, covPolicy)) correctly uses the fresh value — so the legacy's own decision and its own persisted telemetry silently disagree with each other, a one-run-behind defect in the legacy that predates this migration. RunQaUseCase does NOT reproduce this bug: it has no enforce-improvement retry-measure loop at all (a single ObjectiveSignalPort.measure() call site — confirmed by reading the full run-qa.use-case.ts), so it persists the correct value it was given, which for this scenario's dual-engine cross-validation fixture is the post-improvement 1.0 (matching the scenario's own documented intent, not the legacy's buggy telemetry). Root-causing and porting the legacy's stale-binding defect verbatim would be reproducing a known bug for no behavioral benefit — CLAUDE.md's root-cause invariant and this task's own explicit instruction ("Do NOT reproduce the legacy defect") both direct declaring this as an allowlisted, permanent divergence instead of a fix target. Exact root-cause line refs: src/pipeline.ts:2930 (stale assignment) vs src/pipeline.ts:2963 (the reassignment ccForPersistence misses).
